topic.name: Historical Royal Residences and Castles
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant historical buildings used as royal residences or fortifications, highlighting their architectural, political, and social roles throughout history.

Document 1: The Grand Palace is a complex of buildings at the heart of Bangkok, Thailand, and has been the official residence of the Kings of Siam since 1782.
The palace is one of the most popular tourist attractions in Thailand.
Document 2: Ludlow Castle is a ruined medieval fortification in the town of the same name in the English county of Shropshire.
The castle was probably founded by Walter de Lacy after the Norman conquest and was one of the first stone castles to be built in England.
Document 3: Castles have played an important military, economic and social role in Great Britain and Ireland since their introduction following the Norman invasion of England in 1066.
Royal castles were used to control key towns and the economically important forests.

topic.name: Newly Described Fungal Species
topic.description: This topic covers the identification and classification of newly discovered species of fungi across different geographical regions.

Document 1: Pluteus nevadensis is a species of fungus in the agaric family Pluteaceae. Described as new to science in 2010.
The species was described by Olivia Rodríguez in 2010 in the journal Mycotaxon.
Document 2: Pulveroboletus bembae is a species of fungus in the Boletaceae family that was first described in 2009.
The specimens of Pulveroboletus bembae upon which the species description is based were collected in April, 2008.

Document 3: Lactifluus deceptivus, commonly known as the deceiving milkcap, is a common species of fungus in the Russulaceae family.
The species was first described in the scientific...

topic.description: The documents discuss various music tracks and their accompanying videos, highlighting their production, reception, and cultural impact.

topic.name: Strategic Bombers in Military History
topic.description: This topic encompasses the design, development, and operational roles of various strategic bombers used by military forces throughout history.

the backbone of the United Kingdom’s airborne nuclear deterrent during much of the Cold War

designed to carry nuclear weapons for Cold War-era deterrence missions
Document 3: German dive bomber and ground-attack aircraft

topic.name: Cricket History and Players
topic.description: The documents discuss notable cricketers and their contributions to the sport, highlighting their careers and achievements.

Document 1: Neil Harvey is a former Australian cricketer who represented the Australian cricket team between 1948 and 1963.
Harvey was regarded by Wisden as the finest fielder of his era.
Document 2: Irfan Pathan is an Indian cricketer who made his debut for India in the 2003/04 Border-Gavaskar Trophy.
Pathan was named by the International Cricket Council as the 2004 Emerging Player of the Year.

topic.name: U.S. State Highways
topic.description: This topic covers the descriptions and characteristics of major state highways in Maryland and Delaware.

Document 1: Maryland Route 2 is the longest state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
The route runs concurrent with MD 4 through much of Calvert County.
Document 2: U.S. Route 13 is a U.S. highway running from Fayetteville, North Carolina north to Morrisville, Pennsylvania.
US 13 connects many important cities and towns in Delaware.
Document 3: Delaware Route 1 is a 103.02-mile-long state highway in the U.S. state of Delaware.
DE 1 parallels U.S. Route 13, crossing over and featuring interchanges with it multiple times.

known for his charisma and oratorical skills

topic.name: Pre-Dreadnought Battleships
topic.description: The documents discuss the design, service history, and operational roles of various pre-dreadnought battleships from different navies during the late 19th and early 20th centuries.

Document 1: Russian battleship Navarin was a pre-dreadnought battleship built for the Imperial Russian Navy.
She participated in the suppression of the Boxer Rebellion in 1900 before returning to the Baltic Fleet.
Document 2: HMS Triumph was the second of the two Swiftsure-class pre-dreadnought battleships of the Royal Navy.
The ship participated in the hunt for the German East Asia Squadron of Maximilian Graf von Spee.
Document 3: The Braunschweig-class battleships were pre-dreadnought battleships of the Kaiserliche Marine.
The ships saw active service during World War I, primarily as coast defense ships.

topic.name: The Boat Race History
topic.description: The topic encompasses the historical events and significance of the annual rowing competition between the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge.

Document 1: The 68th Boat Race took place on 1 April 1911.
The race was first held in 1829, and since 1845 has taken place on the 4.2-mile Championship Course on the River Thames.
Document 2: The 52nd Boat Race took place on 30 March 1895.
The race was first held in 1829, and since 1845 has taken place on the 4.2-mile Championship Course on the River Thames.
Document 3: The 104th Boat Race took place on 5 April 1958.
The race was first held in 1829, and takes place on the 4.2-mile Championship Course on the River Thames.

topic.name: State Highways in the U.S.
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways in the U.S., detailing their routes, historical context, and intersections with other roads.

Document 1: Delaware Route 300 is an 11.83-mile-long state highway in Kent County, Delaware.
The route was first built as a state highway in the 1920s and 1930s.
Document 2: Delaware Route 26 is a state highway in Sussex County, Delaware.
The road was built as a state highway in various stages during the 1920s and 1930s.
Document 3: State Route 4 is a 62.27-mile-long state highway, serving the southwestern region of the U.S. state of Washington.
The highway is designated as part of the Lewis and Clark Trail Scenic Byway.

topic.name: Film and Cinema
topic.description: The documents discuss notable figures and films in the film industry, highlighting their contributions and significance.

Document 1: Stanley Kubrick was an American film director, screenwriter, producer, cinematographer, editor, and photographer.
Kubrick's films are considered among the most important contributions to world cinema.
Document 2: Frozen is a 2013 American 3D computer-animated musical fantasy comedy film produced by Walt Disney Animation Studios.
It is the 53rd animated feature in the Walt Disney Animated Classics series.
Document 3: Ranganathan Madhavan is an Indian actor, writer and film producer.
Madhavan has received two Filmfare Awards, an award from the Tamil Nadu State Film Awards alongside recognition and nominations from other organisations.

topic.name: Professional Wrestling Careers
topic.description: This topic encompasses the careers and achievements of notable professional wrestlers, highlighting their contributions to the wrestling industry.

Document 1: Shawn Michaels is an American professional wrestling personality, television presenter and retired professional wrestler.
Michaels is a four-time world champion: a three-time WWF Champion and a one-time World Heavyweight Champion.
Document 2: Matt Hardy is an American professional wrestler and wrestling promoter currently signed with Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA).
Hardy is a three-time world champion, having held the TNA World Heavyweight Championship twice and the ECW Championship once.
Document 3: Kurt Angle is an American professional wrestler, actor, former mixed martial artist and retired amateur wrestler.
Angle made his first appearance at a professional wrestling event in 1996, with his in-ring debut following in 1998.

topic.name: Television Episode Analysis
topic.description: The common topic revolves around the analysis of specific episodes from television series, focusing on their plot, character dynamics, and audience reception.

Document 1: The episode depicts the everyday lives of office employees in the Scranton, Pennsylvania branch of the fictional Dunder Mifflin Paper Company.
The episode received largely positive reviews from television critics.
Document 2: The episode is a 'Monster-of-the-Week' story, unconnected to the series' wider mythology.
The episode received mixed reviews from television critics.
Document 3: The series depicts the everyday lives of office employees in the Scranton, Pennsylvania, branch of the fictional Dunder Mifflin Paper Company.
The episode featured several direct references to the Fox series Glee.

topic.name: Insect Ecology and Diversity
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of insect species, their ecological roles, and the significance of biodiversity within ecosystems.

Document 1: Lepidoptera is an order of insects that includes moths and butterflies.
The Lepidoptera show many variations of the basic body structure that have evolved to gain advantages in lifestyle and distribution.
Document 2: Ecology is the scientific analysis and study of interactions among organisms and their environment.
Biodiversity enhances certain ecosystem services.
Document 3: The meat ant is a species of ant endemic to Australia.
Its enormous distribution, aggression and ecological importance have made this ant a dominant species.

topic.name: Arguments for God's Existence
topic.description: This topic encompasses various philosophical and theological arguments that seek to demonstrate the existence of God, including the teleological and ontological arguments, as well as the practices and beliefs of specific Christian denominations.

Document 1: The teleological or physico-theological argument, also known as the argument from design, is an argument for the existence of God based on perceived evidence of deliberate design in the natural or physical world.
The teleological argument has a long association with the Abrahamic religions.
Document 2: Churches of Christ seek to base doctrine and practice on the Bible alone in order to be the church described in the New Testament.
The Restoration ideal was also similar and somewhat connected to earlier restoration efforts in Europe.
Document 3: An ontological argument is a philosophical argument for the existence of God that uses ontology.
The first ontological argument in the Western Christian tradition was proposed by Anselm of Canterbury in his 1078 work Proslogion.

topic.name: Pop Ballads and Themes
topic.description: The common topic revolves around pop ballads that explore themes of love, heartbreak, and personal empowerment.

Document 1: 'Take a Bow' lyrically talks about unrequited love, and Madonna saying goodbye.
'Take a Bow' was developed from this collaboration, after Madonna listened to the beat and the chords of the demo structure of the song.
Document 2: 'Best Thing I Never Had' was composed by Patrick 'J. Que' Smith, Kenneth 'Babyface' Edmonds, and Beyoncé, and explores themes of revenge and karma.
'Best Thing I Never Had' was deemed to have strong radio appeal and was praised for its openness.
Document 3: 'Bad Romance' explores Gaga's attraction to individuals with whom romance never works, her preference for lonely relationships.
'Bad Romance' was touted by Gaga as an experimental pop record.

topic.name: Video Game Consoles and RPGs
topic.description: The documents discuss the evolution of video game consoles and the impact of the Dragon Quest RPG series on the gaming industry.

Document 1: seventh generation includes consoles released since late 2005 by Nintendo, Microsoft, and Sony Computer Entertainment
video game consoles had become an important part of the global IT infrastructure
Document 2: first role-playing video game (RPG) in the Dragon Quest media franchise
Dragon Quest has been ported and remade for several video game platforms
Document 3: series of internationally best-selling console role-playing video game (RPG) titles
the series has had a significant impact on the development of console RPGs

topic.name: Medieval Churches in Wales and England
topic.description: The documents discuss various medieval churches in Wales and England, highlighting their historical significance, architectural features, and current status as places of worship or heritage sites.

Document 1: St Peter's Church, Llanbedrgoch is a small medieval parish church near the village of Llanbedrgoch in Anglesey, north Wales.
It is a Grade II* listed building, a national designation given to 'particularly important buildings of more than special interest'.
Document 2: St Peter's Church is a redundant Anglican church in the town of Sudbury, Suffolk, England.
It is recorded in the National Heritage List for England as a designated Grade I listed building.
Document 3: St Mary's Church, Bodewryd is a small medieval church in the hamlet of Bodewryd, in Anglesey, north Wales.
It is a Grade II listed building, a national designation given to 'buildings of special interest, which warrant every effort being made to preserve them'.

topic.name: Tropical Cyclones and Storms
topic.description: The documents discuss various tropical cyclones, their formation, meteorological history, impacts, and associated damages.

Document 1: Tropical Storm Becky was the second tropical cyclone and named storm of the 1970 Atlantic hurricane season.
Overall, damage was related to heavy rainfall that caused flooding in Tallahassee, Florida.
Document 2: Tropical Storm Alma of the 2008 Pacific hurricane season was the easternmost forming Pacific tropical cyclone on record.
In Costa Rica, heavy rainfall caused flooding and landslides, killing two and causing $35 million (USD) in damage.
Document 3: Hurricane Caroline was one of two tropical cyclones to affect northern Mexico during the 1975 Atlantic hurricane season.
There were only two deaths from Caroline, both indirect.

topic.name: Military Engagements in History
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant military engagements and formations from historical conflicts, particularly focusing on the American Revolutionary War and the First World War.

Document 1: The Battles of Lexington and Concord were the first military engagements of the American Revolutionary War.
The battles marked the outbreak of open armed conflict between the Kingdom of Great Britain and thirteen of its colonies.
Document 2: The Auckland Mounted Rifles Regiment was a mounted infantry regiment from New Zealand raised for service during the First World War.
The regiment fought predominantly against the forces of the Ottoman Turkish Empire.
Document 3: The Wellington Mounted Rifles Regiment was a mounted infantry regiment from New Zealand, raised for service during the First World War.
The regiment fought predominantly against the forces of the Ottoman Turkish Empire.

topic.name: NCAA College Football Seasons
topic.description: The documents discuss various NCAA college football teams and their performances during specific seasons, highlighting key players, coaching staff, and game outcomes.

Document 1: The 2001 Gator Bowl was a post-season American college football bowl game between the Virginia Tech Hokies and the Clemson Tigers
Virginia Tech entered the game headed by star quarterback Michael Vick
Document 2: The 2012 Alabama Crimson Tide football team represented the University of Alabama in the 2012 National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) Division I Football Bowl Subdivision (FBS) football season
They finished the season with a record of 13 wins and 1 loss (13 – 1 overall, 7 – 1 in the SEC)
Document 3: The 2013 Penn State Nittany Lions football team represented Pennsylvania State University in the 2013 NCAA Division I FBS football season
Penn State was ineligible to play in a bowl game for the 2013 season, the second season of a four-year ban

topic.name: Pacific Hurricane and Typhoon Seasons
topic.description: The documents discuss various Pacific hurricane and typhoon seasons, highlighting their activity levels, storm formations, and impacts.

Document 1: 1990 Pacific hurricane season is the fifth most active season on record
Overall, the impact of this season was minimal.
Document 2: The 1983 Pacific hurricane season was the longest season ever recorded at that time
During the 1983 season, there were 21 named storms.
Document 3: The 1994 Pacific typhoon season was an extremely active season
The season had no official bounds and it ran year-round in 1994.

topic.name: Hungarian Monarchs History
topic.description: The documents discuss the reigns and significant events related to various kings of Hungary, highlighting their political struggles and contributions to the kingdom's development.

Document 1: Andrew II ruled Hungary and Croatia between 1205 and 1235.
Andrew's rule was unpopular, and the boyars expelled him.
Document 2: Stephen V was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1270 and 1272.
A civil war broke out between Stephen and his father, King Béla IV.
Document 3: Andrew I strengthened the position of Christianity in the Kingdom of Hungary.
Andrew I was dethroned by his brother, Béla, in 1060.

topic.name: George Harrison and The Beatles
topic.description: This topic encompasses the solo career of George Harrison and his contributions to The Beatles, focusing on their music, themes, and the dynamics within the band.

Document 1: 'My Sweet Lord' topped charts worldwide and was the biggest-selling single of 1971 in the UK.
Harrison wrote 'My Sweet Lord' in praise of the Hindu god Krishna.
Document 2: The Beatles, also known as the White Album, is the ninth studio album by English rock group the Beatles.
Most of the songs on the album were written during March and April 1968 at a Transcendental Meditation course in Rishikesh, India.
Document 3: All Things Must Pass is a triple album by English musician George Harrison.
The album reflects the influence of his musical activities with artists such as Bob Dylan, the Band, Delaney & Bonnie and Billy Preston during 1968 – 70.

topic.name: Fictional Television Characters
topic.description: The documents discuss various fictional characters from popular television series and soap operas, highlighting their roles, relationships, and development over time.

Document 1: FBI special agents Fox Mulder and Dana Scully who investigate X-Files
Mulder believes in the existence of aliens and the paranormal while Scully, a medical doctor and a skeptic, is assigned to make scientific analyses.
Document 2: Mercedes Maria Theresa Immaculata McQueen is a fictional character from the British Channel 4 soap opera Hollyoaks
Mercedes is part of the McQueen family and is the longest serving McQueen on the series.
Document 3: Susan Wendy Kennedy is a fictional character from the Australian soap opera Neighbours
Susan has become a stepmother and a grandmother and she has been central to three plots revolving around health issues.
